text.text:
### Images from UNESCO

These images are copyright for UNESCO and allowed to be used according to the license determined by them.

To visualize all images, use the left and right arrows on the slide below.

1 / 5
Archaeological National Site of L'Anse aux Meadows. Photo by Dick Houghton, available on the UNESCO website (UNESCO, ). Shared under license Attribution-Noncommercial 2.0 Generic.
2 / 5
Altamira Cave and Paleolithic Cave Art of Northern Spain. Photo by Yvon Fruneau. © UNESCO (UNESCO, 12/21/2008). Shared under the Creative Commons Attribution-ShareAlike 3.0 IGO license.
3 / 5
Great Zimbabwe National Monument (The National Museum and Monuments of Zimbabwe, 02/01/2024). Photograph taken by Erik Eschweiler-Dienerowitz and made available on the UNESCO website (UNESCO, 12/21/2025). Shared under the Creative Commons Attribution-ShareAlike 3.0 IGO license.
4 / 5
Great Wall of China. Photo by Giovanni Boccardi, available on the UNESCO website (UNESCO, 12/25/2024). Shared under license Creative Commons Attribution-ShareAlike 3.0 IGO.
5 / 5
Behistun Inscription. Photo taken by Babak Sedighi and made available on the UNESCO website (Sedighi, Babak, 07/10/2006, UNESCO). Shared under the Creative Commons Attribution-ShareAlike 3.0 IGO license.

### Image Descriptions
- **Slide 1 / 5**: Archaeological National Site of L'Anse aux Meadows. Photo by Dick Houghton, available on the UNESCO website (UNESCO, ). Shared under license Attribution-Noncommercial 2.0 Generic.
- **Slide 2 / 5**: Altamira Cave and Paleolithic Cave Art of Northern Spain. Photo by Yvon Fruneau. © UNESCO (*UNESCO, 12/21/2008*). Shared under the Creative Commons Attribution-ShareAlike 3.0 IGO license.
- **Slide 3 / 5**: Great Zimbabwe National Monument (*The National Museum and Monuments of Zimbabwe, 02/01/2024*). Photograph taken by Erik Eschweiler-Dienerowitz and made available on the UNESCO website (*UNESCO, 12/21/2025*). Shared under the Creative Commons Attribution-ShareAlike 3.0 IGO license.
- **Slide 4 / 5**: Great Wall of China. Photo by Giovanni Boccardi, available on the UNESCO website (*UNESCO, 12/25/2024*). Shared under license Creative Commons Attribution-ShareAlike 3.0 IGO.
- **Slide 5 / 5**: Behistun Inscription. Photo taken by Babak Sedighi and made available on the UNESCO website (*Sedighi, Babak, 07/10/2006, UNESCO*). Shared under the Creative Commons Attribution-ShareAlike 3.0 IGO license.
